hook.text: In 1967, two ecologists proposed a simple idea that reshaped conservation forever.

buildup.text: MacArthur and Wilson argued that island species richness balances immigration against extinction.

discovery.text: Close islands get more immigrants. Big islands have fewer extinctions. Both get more species.

twist.text: The theory predicted species numbers without knowing which species—just size and distance.

climax.text: It became the foundation for designing nature reserves as habitat islands on land.

punchline.text: Size and distance predict diversity. That's the rule.

quiz.question: What two factors determine island species richness in the equilibrium theory?
options: Island size and distance from the mainland
